questions.q: What are the essential characteristics of Group 1 elements?
questions.answer: The essential characteristics of Group 1 elements are that they are highly reactive metals with one electron in their outermost shell. They have a low ionization energy and tend to lose one electron to form a positive ion with a +1 charge. They have a high reactivity and tend to react with water to produce hydrogen gas and alkaline solutions.

questions.q: What is the principle behind the periodic arrangement of s-block elements?
questions.answer: The periodic arrangement of s-block elements is based on the increase in atomic number and the number of electrons in the outermost shell. The elements with the same number of electrons in the outermost shell exhibit similar chemical properties and are placed in the same group.
key_points: Number of electrons in outermost shell

questions.q: Why do the alkali metals tend to lose one electron to form a positive ion?
questions.answer: The alkali metals tend to lose one electron to form a positive ion because they have one electron in their outermost shell, which is easily lost to achieve a stable noble gas configuration.
key_points: Easily lost to achieve stable configuration

questions.q: What are the common uses of sodium and potassium?
questions.topic: Occurrence and Uses of s-block elements
questions.answer: Sodium and potassium are commonly used in the manufacture of soap, paper, and other cellulose products. They are also used in the production of chlorine gas and caustic soda.

questions.q: Why are the s-block elements highly reactive?
questions.answer: The s-block elements are highly reactive because they have one or two electrons in their outermost shell, which are easily lost to form a positive ion and achieve a stable noble gas configuration.
key_points: One or two electrons in outermost shell
Easily lost to achieve stable configuration

questions.q: What is the effect of pressure on the melting point of s-block elements?
questions.answer: The melting point of s-block elements decreases with an increase in pressure. This is because the atoms are forced closer together, reducing the intermolecular forces and making it easier for the solid to melt.

questions.q: Why are the s-block elements good conductors of electricity?
questions.answer: The s-block elements are good conductors of electricity because they are highly reactive and tend to lose one electron to form a positive ion. This results in the presence of free electrons in the solid, which can move easily under the influence of an electric field.

questions.q: What is the difference between sodium and potassium in terms of their chemical properties?
questions.answer: Sodium and potassium exhibit similar chemical properties, but potassium is more reactive than sodium due to its smaller size and higher ionization energy. Potassium also reacts more vigorously with water to produce hydrogen gas and heat.

questions.q: Why is lithium used as a battery material?
questions.topic: Occurrence and Uses of s-block elements
questions.answer: Lithium is used as a battery material because it has a high electrochemical potential and can easily lose one electron to form a positive ion. This results in a high energy density and long battery life.

questions.q: What is the principle behind the extraction of sodium from its ore?
questions.topic: Occurrence and Extraction of s-block elements
questions.answer: The principle behind the extraction of sodium from its ore is the electrolysis of a molten salt, such as sodium chloride. The sodium ion is reduced at the cathode to form sodium metal, while the chlorine gas is released at the anode.
